evals.prompt: audit the components in my project and tell me how to organize them
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report classifying each component by atomic level, identifying mixed-level directories, dependency direction violations, and misplaced screen components.

assertions.text: Correctly identifies framework (React/Expo) and UI library (shadcn/NativeWind)
Identifies atom-level components (Button, Input, Badge, etc.)
Identifies organism-level components (forms, cards with actions, headers)
Flags directories containing mixed atomic levels
Identifies screen components in ui/atoms folders as misplaced
Output follows structured report with inventory table and recommendations
Includes specific move/restructure actions with file paths

evals.prompt: I need to build a PlayerComparisonCard that shows two player avatars, names, and stats side by side — where should I put this?
evals.expected_output: Classification as organism with clear reasoning and exact file path placement.

assertions.text: Correctly classifies PlayerComparisonCard as an organism
Explains why it's not a molecule (4+ elements, multiple concerns)
Identifies constituent atoms/molecules (Avatar, Text, StatRow)
Provides exact file path (components/sections/ or features/*/components/)
Lists what it should import and what should import it
References or applies the decision flowchart logic

evals.prompt: set up atomic design folder structure for my Expo project that uses NativeWind and shadcn-rn
evals.expected_output: Scaffold using shadcn-rn conventions: ui/ for atoms, composed/ for molecules, sections/ for organisms, templates/ for layouts.

assertions.text: Detects shadcn-rn and adapts naming (ui/composed/sections vs atoms/molecules/organisms)
Keeps components/ui/ as-is for atoms (shadcn primitives)
Creates components/composed/ for molecules
Creates components/sections/ for organisms
Creates components/templates/ for layouts
Does not create a pages/ folder — app/ routes are pages
Explains feature-specific components stay in features/
